'''Categorization | Class Lambda-VII'''
'''》Designation | Pestis'''
'''》Domain | Virgo Supercluster'''
==Description==
[[File:The Scourge.png]] An artistic depiction of its appearance.
The purported wielder of the Jawbreaker is an entity known as '''The Scourge'''. '''The Scourge''' is a being of significant and frightening strength, possessing the appearance of a disgustingly disfigured and decaying goat-like humanoid. It exudes a horrific, pungent stench comparable to the scent of rotten flesh. Protruding from its head are various body parts belonging to a multitude of organisms, such as its disproportionate tongue and jutting crooked horns. These body parts do not seem to have originally belonged to the entity, as its horns visibly pierce through its flesh rather than stemming neatly from its head. This effect is likely due to the Jawbreaker's curse. '''The Scourge''''s capabilities and influence are of terrifying caliber, capable of crumbling the most advanced civilizations beyond repair. The entity seems to be ostensibly inclined toward the immediate execution of its victims, being described as having a cruel, sadistic personality, as it is seemingly amused by the terror and pain it brings upon its victims. As such, '''The Scourge''' typically attempts to prolong the process of draining one's life, inflicting severe pain upon its victims before their untimely demise. Additionally, the entity's apparent destructive abilities have been observed to be capable of reaching even heavenly bodies, such as planets and stars. If the entity were to hypothetically unleash all of its energy at once, decimation would be brought upon hundreds of galaxies, leaving not even space in their place. Its primary method of causing widespread destruction is unleashing a powerful discharge of energy. Such energy is obtained via the absorption of the organic matter of its victims combined with consumed star matter, which it subsequently converts into usable material power, releasing or storing it. The entity cannot speak or communicate due to the curse of its artifact. Attempts to persuade the entity to communicate have proven futile, but it has demonstrated the capacity to understand the intentions of those that wish to communicate. Under no circumstances should communication be attempted with '''The Scourge'''; regardless of the apparent safety measures one may have, its violent, tactical nature often leaves its victims little more than dust. ==Behaviors== Attempting to record or predict '''The Scourge''''s actions has proven arduous, commonly resulting in inaccurate reports of its behavior. Its aforementioned sadistic personality is the most noticeable quality of the entity, as well as its immense knowledge of the concept of pain, such as seemingly understanding the exact areas of the body that contain the highest number of nerve endings, notably the fingertips and forehead, with the entity typically inflicting deep incisions in those areas whilst avoiding significant bodily damage to prolong the victim's life and thus prolong their pain. The behavior of '''The Scourge''' typically leads to it initially being labeled as a mindless beast, one that relies on pure instinct and bloodlust. However, this has been shown to simply be a façade artificially constructed by the entity to increase the trauma brought upon its victims. Its lust for pain is difficult to comprehend, as its obsession reaches unfathomable bounds, going as far as the aforementioned extension of its victims' lives to prolong their torture. Survival is nigh-impossible upon encountering the entity, as even minuscule amounts of unnatural noise can trigger it to attack. While one may indeed survive an attack by the entity given the right circumstances, the very few individuals that have survived its attacks are left in critical condition, being permanently crippled and traumatized. To date, only three victims have ever been left alive, albeit left in a comatose state, as the others have ended their own lives afterward due to the immense mental trauma inflicted upon them. Despite '''The Scourge''''s complete inability to communicate, its knowledge and intelligence of empathy undoubtedly surpass those of any human; it has shown insurmountable levels of skill in manipulating the past trauma of victims to increase their inflicted pain, and in separating physical and mental torture to presumably increase their respective effectiveness. Its unmatched intelligence is likely due to its extraterrestrial origins and dissimilar environment. It is primarily active within the Virgo Supercluster, and all lower-dimensional spaces are connected to that area of baseline reality; this includes the clusters of the Backrooms related to many intelligent races, including the human race. ==Biology== As aforementioned, '''The Scourge''''s body is an amalgamated combination of various body parts seemingly originating from other beings, with it having several disproportionate horns, tongues, ears, and orifices strewn about its fleshy body. Its head contains the majority of the conglomerated extremities, with its horns marking its inevitable presence. In addition to the amount of damage its extremities are capable of causing to others, its body is exceptionally powerful. It is capable of destroying skulls with a mere flick of the hand, and has demonstrated immense durability against heavy firepower and ammunition with no visible signs of damage. Due to this, one should never engage in combat against '''The Scourge''', as its unnaturally high durability paired with its destructive capabilities make it virtually unbeatable. Of note, pieces of dropped flesh from its body should not be touched, as the disease held within is highly infectious, with all currently-tested methods of cure failing to exterminate it from a victim's body. Contact with '''The Scourge''' will immediately cause one's body to disintegrate instantly, leaving no discernible trace whatsoever. The destruction seemingly applies down to even the molecular level, as not a single living cell has been detected during autopsies of deceased victims. The cause of this disintegration appears to be the spores regularly released by the entity; simply making contact with a minute amount of such spores typically causes one's body to undergo an entire internal collapse. The entity has occasionally demonstrated control over the spores' severity; as mentioned before, it will typically weaken the inflicted decay to a noticeable degree, slowing it enough to allow the victims to live up to several hours before their death. Its aforementioned inability to communicate is the most mysterious feature of the entity; '''The Scourge''' is fully capable of producing sound, yet for reasons that, as of yet, remain unknown, it is unable to or refuses to use them for purposes of communication. Furthermore, it is unable to use items that allow writing and typing because hypothetical attempts will cause the item to decay into nothingness; as a result, any possible mode(s) of communication are deemed out of its capability. ==='''Ancient Record'''=== The following records describe events that led up to the entity's obtainment of the Jawbreaker, these records are mostly validated, thought cannot be entire proven due to the lack of knowledge about the entity. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial=''' > VIEW RECORD 1 |final=''' > RECORD 1 OPENED '''|body=
 
Tyranny's End
Before the inevitable creation of other civilizations, on the brink of extinction was a species of intergalactic conquerors known as the "Pestilence Arbiters". These humanoid, authoritative monsters possessed the ability to spread a disease through the emission of spores, one that would slowly decay one's body upon contact. Each member of its society had different effects and severity of decay, but even the weakest were unbeatable by most. This terrifying capability allowed them to assert dominance over other species, growing their power into immense magnitudes, until they eventually reached the proverbial status of gods. The heavens were nothing out of their reach—planets and stars were considered disposable. However, unbeknownst to them, observers already had judgment in mind. Civilizations further away from their galaxy deemed the beings to have surpassed their upper limit; fearing one of them would come to discover an artifact, hunted down the entities fiercely. A war had broken out, one that destroyed planets and several solar systems. The numbers proved to be too much to handle for the Pestilence Arbiters, leading them to steadily decline into extinction. As their population decreased, their tyranny slowly dissipated over the course of eons. Many gleefully thought that these monsters had finally finished their long-lasting period of ruling and had lost their power, but this would prove to be false with time.
Few had survived the event, with those survivors eventually growing into a rebellious group of less than a hundred. Of these members, a unique one stood out. This specific member had the most severe decaying effect out of all the others, to the point of lacking control over the ability entirely, something that had never occurred before. The cost of its power, however, was an unending and insatiable need to spread the spores to victims. In the eyes of others, this effect was considered a weakness, as its lack of control made him virtually useless in aiding fights against the competition. It would often wander out of the conurbation's perimeters, scorning over its outcast status. This continued for weeks, months, and a year until a certain day of fate arrived. It suddenly fell to the ground, sinking through it; as the members viewed this phenomenon and started frantically searching for the lost being, their efforts would prove fruitless, for it had ended up elsewhere. Bewildered and confused, it looked up only to see [[Level 0|a labyrinth of yellow.]]
}} ---- {{Collapse|num=2|initial=''' > VIEW RECORD 2 |final=''' > RECORD 2 OPENED '''|body=
 
The Beauty of Silence
The entity stood up and searched frantically for a non-existent exit, before eventually succumbing to exhaustion. Disheveled, it began crying out of hopelessness, before encountering a Hound. Terrified, it ran away to safety, but the Hound proved to be faster. Its running and panting got closer and closer until he felt it touch his leg. Out of instinct, he attempted to remove the Hound's hand; however, the moment its hand made contact with the Hound, it immediately decayed into a small pile of dust, leaving no trace of its previous existence. The event surprised the entity, almost making it fall back. Heavy breathing filled the environment around it; for the first time, it finally had clarity and peace. The silence overjoyed it. Before it could savor the moment, the entity again heard another noise approaching. Fearing the silence would disappear and it would get discovered, it ran away at a surprisingly blinding speed. It ran, again and again, attempting to find silence, yet wherever it went, the noise would follow. Hours passed, no wall being devoid of sound, until it suddenly saw the yellow halls around it warp into another environment entirely; stopped in its tracks, it looked around to see where it was, finding itself in a massive warehouse. As it continued its search for silence, it discovered further shifting of environments, changing again and again. Eventually, the entity became aware of the concept of noclipping. It knew silence would be somewhere out there, where everything could finally be normal; as such, it started a quest to seek it.
}} ---- {{Collapse|num=3|initial=''' > VIEW RECORD 3 |final=''' > RECORD 3 OPENED '''|body=
 
Despair of Godhood
Years passed, and its search for silence became an obsession; the flow of time became a dissipating concept. A sense of bloodlust dominated its disarrayed mind — its aggression grew alarmingly quick, morphing it into a cold, psychotic, desperate being, begging fate to give it silence. As it stumbled and hit a leaning structure, it again noticed a shift in the environment, though this [[Level 94|new area]] exuded a different feeling. The silence and lack of life were exactly what it had searched for, but despite what it had hoped, it did not feel joy. Radiating from somewhere in the level was a sinister, yet surprisingly pleasant energy, though the source was unclear. Its desire to discover its source had replaced its previous lust for silence; with all its power, it sped across the level. Though this process took hours, wherever he went, the source never seemed to be nearer. As it started to end its search for the source, everything suddenly disappeared. This was starkly different from the typical experience of noclipping, though, as the surrounding darkness remained there. All lasting light dissipated, leaving it engulfed in a formless, lightless mass akin to a black hole. There was simply nothing, not a molecule of matter in sight. It felt a sudden, excruciating pain — the mass engulfing the entity seemed to be attempting to absorb it, its matter being torn away. The entity's power prevented it from being fully absorbed by the mass, however, leaving it in a painful cycle of constant devouring and repair. Perhaps this unseen devouring force was the cause of the stark lack of matter around it. Thirteen days of agonizing torture, constant cellular destruction, weakened the previously almighty entity. Slowly losing its strength, the entity did not believe the pain would end, convinced its death would be near; however, the instant it lost its strength to continue fighting the destructive spatial body, the entity suddenly fell, landing on pristine ground, fatigued from its unending efforts of survival. Towering over the dying entity was an indescribable temple, radiating the sinister energy it had felt just thirteen days prior. It gathered its remaining might and used it to traverse the temple's enormous interior, walking down a large, grandiose hall akin to a church gateway. Continuing its journey into the subterranean section of the main hall, it neared the source of the energy, where it found an impressive sight. Showcased in front of the entity was a breathtaking collection of eleven objects — an enormous Trident, a beautiful Scepter, a sphere inundated with fractal-like patterns. The sinister energy of the sphere attracted the entity, unknowing of its power. It weakly approached the artifact, soon making contact with it in an attempt to take it. As the sheer power of the process of an artifact's power transferring into a living host is physically incomprehensible, it is unknown what happened thereafter; however, whatever happened inside, whatever happened to The Scourge — be it good or bad — the artifact evidently took a toll on it.  Exiting the temple's main hall, an unrecognizable beast made itself seen. Its fractured skull, destroyed jaw, and rotting flesh made it apparent that it was no longer the being that had initially entered — it was The Scourge. In the palm of its hand was a sphere of fractal-like patterns — The Jawbreaker — making it clear that The Scourge successfully absorbed its power. Its sheer aura and power proved to be unfathomable; its decay had surpassed the laws of space, no longer requiring contact in order to annihilate its target. The entity initially did not wish to be disturbed, thinking to stay inside the impenetrable temple, but it soon changed its mind, overtaken by a lust for blood.
}} ---- The records shown above were recovered from within the confines of [[Level 94]]'s temple. Their validity is yet to be fully proven, but their abundant and verifiable details give them merit. The records are theorized to have been written by an ancient civilization, approximately a Type 1 on the Kardashev scale. Overall, the records' origins and truthfulness are not fully known, nor fully validated due to the lack of necessary knowledge to obtain said information.
